summary: Clean, behavior-preserving refactor that resolves tech-debt [004] by exporting the procpidstat IO/iodelay column-index constants from internal/stat and removing the duplicated local const block in report.go. Constant values (9/10/11) match the 0-based order of procPidResultCols exactly, all references were migrated (no leftover dead refs), there is no import-cycle risk (report->stat is one-way), and build/vet/test all pass.

suggestions.suggestion: The doc-comment correctly states these constants must stay in sync with procPidResultCols, but the invariant is only enforced by a code comment. Consider an optional compile-time or test-time assertion (e.g. a small test asserting procPidResultCols[ColReadTotalKiB] == "read_total,KiB", etc.) so a future column reorder fails loudly rather than silently shifting indices.
suggestions.benefit: Turns the textual 'must match' contract into an automatically verified guarantee, protecting against a silent regression if the column slice is reordered.
